name: Little Tarts (British)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift flour and salt together.
2. Make a small well in the center of the flour, and pour egg and water into it.
3. Combine all ingredients, mixing and kneading until a smooth, elastic dough is formed (about 20 minutes).
4. Allow dough to sit for about 20 minutes.
5. Divide dough in half. On a lightly floured surface, roll out halves one at a time into sheets almost paper thin. Dough will be elastic, and much determination is required in this process. Place a section of the dough over the side of the table as you roll to ease the task.
6. Cut each sheet of dough into about 10 or 12 rectangular pieces.
FOR THE FILLING AND BROTH
1. Beat eggs, spices, and salt together.
2. Blend egg mixture, pork, and currants.
3. Place a teaspoon of stuffing on each piece of dough.
4. Beat the third egg. Run this "paste" along the edges of each piece of dough.
5. Fold dough over stuffing so edges meet. Seal by pressing edges together.
6. Bring stock to boil. Add tartlettes and any leftover stuffing, reducing heat to a gentle boil.
7. Cook about 10 to 12 minutes or until noodle dough reaches consistency you enjoy in spaghetti.
